# Emoji codes

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> `test/stories/emoji_codes_story_test.exs`. Every scenario below is an ExUnit test driving the
> real Codemojex game, so the catalogue cannot drift from the code.

**6 scenarios.**

## Scenario: an XXYY code is column-then-row and round-trips

- **Given** the code "0305"
- **Then** it addresses column 3, row 5, and code/2 is the inverse of xy/1

## Scenario: a code maps to the exact sprite-sheet offset the frontend renders

- **Given** a 6x6 set at the default 144px cell size
- **Then** code "0305" sits at (-432, -720) pixels

## Scenario: the grid expands to every cell row-major when no subset is given

- **Given** a 6x6 set with no explicit codes
- **Then** the keyboard has all 36 cells and each is a valid code

## Scenario: a well-formed secret is six distinct in-set codes

- **Given** a set and a drawn secret
- **Then** the drawn secret validates, but duplicates and out-of-set codes do not

## Scenario: a secret drawn from a reduced N-cell snapshot stays within that snapshot

- **Given** a randomized 24-cell snapshot of a larger keyboard
- **When** a secret is drawn from the snapshot
- **Then** the six secret codes are distinct and all within the 24-cell snapshot

## Scenario: the player-facing snapshot carries the keyboard but nothing the secret leaks from

- **Given** an emoji set
- **When** the set is snapshotted for the client
- **Then** the snapshot is exactly the keyboard fields — no secret could be derived from it
